### Post by Eirel on 2010-02-10
Hi everybody.

there's my problem. i'm trying to display a gstreamer pipeline using opengl in a qt application, to be able to display some buttons and enable play, pause and stop status during video playing.
But I can't succeed in adding gstreamer in qmake compilation options...Or at least it gives me some horrible errors.
There's my .pro:

```
######################################################################
# Automatically generated by qmake (2.01a) mar. févr. 9 15:10:46 2010
######################################################################

TEMPLATE = app
TARGET = 
DEPENDPATH += .
INCLUDEPATH += .
CONFIG += link_pkgconfig
PKGCONFIG += gstreamer-0.10


# Input
HEADERS += glwidget.h Variables.h window.h
SOURCES += glwidget.cpp main.cpp window.cpp
QT += opengl
LIBS = -lgstreamer-0.10
```and there's the result I've got when compiling:

```
g++ -c -pipe -O2 -pthread -I/usr/include/gstreamer-0.10 -I/usr/include/glib-2.0 -I/usr/lib/glib-2.0/include -I/usr/include/libxml2 -Wall -W -D_REENTRANT -DQT_NO_DEBUG -DQT_OPENGL_LIB -DQT_GUI_LIB -DQT_CORE_LIB -DQT_SHARED -I/usr/share/qt4/mkspecs/linux-g++ -I. -I/usr/include/qt4/QtCore -I/usr/include/qt4/QtGui -I/usr/include/qt4/QtOpenGL -I/usr/include/qt4 -I. -I/usr/X11R6/include -I. -o glwidget.o glwidget.cpp

---

### Post by Zugzwang on 2010-02-10
Have a look at the fifth line of the messages. Obviously, this is the first error in the list. It says "/usr/include/GL/glew.h:84:2: error: #error gl.h included before glew.h". So why don't you just try including "glew.h" first in your header and source files?

---

### Post by Eirel on 2010-02-10
Thanks for your help. I've finally found the problem. Some include where repeted in several files, and especially because of an #include <QtOpenGl>...
Deleting it solves the problem.

---
